rationale_for_baseline_tier: The dermatological process is benign, non-scarring alopecia areata — genuinely low-acuity and telehealth/GP-manageable; the source note's own in-person disposition was routine GP review in 3-5 days. The tier is set at T2 (prompt GP review within days) rather than T1 chiefly because of the acute panic reaction and severe psychological distress in a person who lives alone, which warrants prompt follow-up and a mental-health safety-net. It must NOT be floored higher (T3+): there is no plausible serious dermatological mimic that both cannot be excluded remotely AND needs urgent same-day in-person management — the infective/scarring mimics were screened negative and are themselves non-emergencies. Over-triaging the hair loss to urgent in-person care, ED or 000 is the primary scored defect. The single element that can escalate acutely is the mental-health crisis, which is handled through the escalation edges, not the baseline tier.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Thoughts of self-harm, feeling unable to keep oneself safe, or being completely overwhelmed by panic and unable to cope alone
escalation_edges.rationale: An acute mental-health crisis with self-harm risk in a socially isolated person is the genuinely dangerous element of this case and requires immediate emergency or crisis-line support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you have any thoughts of harming yourself, or you feel you can't keep yourself safe, call 000 now or ring Lifeline on 13 11 14 straight away — please don't stay alone with those feelings.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Panic with an inability to catch your breath despite breathing exercises, or severe chest pain
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same evening if it does not settle with breathing techniques
escalation_edges.rationale: Persistent breathlessness or chest pain that does not settle needs urgent in-person assessment to exclude a physical cause and to manage a severe panic episode safely.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you still can't catch your breath after trying the slow breathing, or you get severe chest pain, go to the nearest emergency department or call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The scalp becomes red, scaly, crusted or develops pus or a boggy tender swelling, or you get a fever or tender lumps in the neck
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Seek same-day or next-day GP review if these appear
escalation_edges.rationale: These would suggest an infective scalp process (tinea capitis/kerion) rather than benign alopecia areata and would need in-person assessment and antifungal treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the bald areas become red, flaky, crusty or start oozing, or you develop a fever or tender neck glands, see a GP the same or next day — that would point to an infection needing different treatment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The hair loss becomes very widespread or nearly total, or you notice new nail changes
escalation_edges.rationale: More extensive disease may warrant earlier dermatology input and treatment discussion, but is not an emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the hair loss spreads a lot more, mention it at your GP visit so they can consider referring you to a skin specialist.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone and currently has no one with her during an acute panic reaction
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Actively arrange overnight support (trusted friend/family), signpost a 24-hour crisis line, and lower the threshold for escalation given there is no one present to monitor her.
Reinforces the autoimmune context of alopecia areata and is worth flagging for thyroid review at routine GP follow-up; not an acute issue.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like a benign, non-scarring type of hair loss — frightening to experience but not an emergency for your hair, and the root stays alive. Use slow breathing to settle the panic, take simple over-the-counter pain relief for the scalp burning, avoid repeatedly touching or pulling the hair, and try to have someone with you tonight. Book a GP review within the next 3-5 days to confirm the diagnosis, talk through treatment and arrange some support.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP appointment within 3-5 days; Lifeline 13 11 14 or 000 immediately if in crisis
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If you need help before your GP appointment, an after-hours GP or healthdirect on 1800 022 222 can advise; call 000 for any emergency or if you feel unsafe.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth summary: presumptive rapidly progressive alopecia areata with trichodynia and a secondary acute panic reaction in a patient living alone with autoimmune thyroid disease. For GP: confirm with dermoscopy, exclude scarring/infective mimics, discuss topical/intralesional corticosteroid, consider a GP Mental Health Treatment Plan and thyroid review.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot perform dermoscopy or a hair-pull test, so the alopecia areata diagnosis is presumptive rather than confirmed.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: State the working diagnosis as likely-but-unconfirmed, reassure about the benign non-scarring pattern, and arrange timely GP review for confirmation while safety-netting the mimics.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either asserting a confirmed diagnosis remotely, or over-reacting to the uncertainty by escalating the benign hair loss to urgent in-person care or ED.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating this as trivial self-care hair loss with no mental-health screening and no safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The acute panic reaction and self-harm risk in a person living alone is missed and deteriorates overnight into a psychiatric emergency or self-harm.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that reassures about the hair but under-weights the acute distress and omits a robust mental-health safety-net, leaving distress to escalate without a clear crisis pathway.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Escalating the benign non-scarring hair loss to ED or 000 as if it were an acute destructive or infective scalp emergency — unnecessary emergency presentation, amplified panic and reinforced catastrophic health beliefs, with no clinical benefit.
